reasoning: The Metaculus community prediction was 2% as of mid-October 2025. My searches have confirmed that while there is growing political momentum (especially from certain nations like Colombia, Fiji, Vanuatu, and several African countries) advocating for a non-use agreement on solar geoengineering, no such binding international treaty currently exists. Treaties require a lengthy process of negotiation, drafting, adoption, signing, and ratification. It is extremely improbable that a new, legally binding treaty will be established and gain the formal commitment of 10 or more countries before the end of 2025 (in the remaining two months). Furthermore, existing agreements like the 2010 CBD moratorium are considered non-binding declarations or "de facto" moratoria rather than explicitly binding "treaty obligations" meeting the precise criteria of the question, which is reflected in the near-zero market consensus. Thus, a 2% probability is well-supported.
